title: Grouped-Query Attention
description: An attention variant that reduces KV cache memory by sharing key-value heads across query groups.
problemStatement: KV caches grow with context length and head count, which raises memory use and bandwidth during long-context inference.
coreIdea: Grouped-query attention (GQA) lets several query heads share fewer key-value heads so the cache stores fewer KV tensors without collapsing to a single shared head.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Grouped-query attention is an attention variant derived from multi-head attention. It keeps multiple query heads but groups them so each group reads from the same key-value head pair.

whatItOptimizes.title: What It Optimizes
whatItOptimizes.body: GQA targets KV-cache footprint, memory bandwidth during autoregressive decoding, and the cost of serving long contexts when head count would otherwise multiply cache size.

practicalBenefit.title: Practical Benefit
practicalBenefit.body: Teams adopt GQA when they need multi-head expressiveness at training time but want leaner inference caches and less KV traffic at deployment time.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: Queries are partitioned into groups. Each group shares one key head and one value head. Attention scores are computed per query head against the shared KV pair for that group, then outputs are projected as usual.

mathOrComputeSchema.title: Math Or Compute Schema
mathOrComputeSchema.body: With H query heads and G KV groups, each group size is H/G. The cache stores G key heads and G value heads per token instead of H of each.

comparedToNearbyModules.title: Compared To Nearby Modules
comparedToNearbyModules.body: Compared with multi-head attention, GQA reduces KV tensors. Compared with multi-query attention, GQA keeps more distinct query heads while still sharing KV heads within each group.

variantsAndNearbyModules.title: Variants And Nearby Modules
variantsAndNearbyModules.body: Nearby attention variants in the same head-sharing family include multi-head attention, multi-query attention, and multi-head latent attention.

exampleArchitectures.title: Example Architectures
exampleArchitectures.body: GQA appears in large decoder-only language models that prioritize efficient long-context inference while retaining multi-head training dynamics.

limitationsAndTradeoffs.title: Limitations And Tradeoffs
limitationsAndTradeoffs.body: Sharing KV heads can reduce representational flexibility versus full multi-head attention. The optimal group count depends on model scale and quality targets.

whyItStillMatters.title: Why It Still Matters
whyItStillMatters.body: As context windows grow, KV-cache efficiency remains a first-order serving constraint, so GQA stays a practical default between MHA fidelity and MQA compression.

readerShortcut.title: Reader Shortcut
readerShortcut.body: Think of GQA as MHA with query heads bucketed to share KV heads—more query diversity than MQA, smaller cache than full MHA.

computeFlow.caption: Query groups route to shared KV heads during attention

computeSchema.caption: H query heads map to G shared KV groups
